title: The Pattern
timelinePosition: Directly follows Chapter 5's route choice. The party is already on the southern feeder road that runs near Strahnbrad before the final Andorhal approach.
openLocation: Southern feeder road into Andorhal's network; Strahnbrad military zone ahead.
immediateObjective: Confirm the scale of the grain conspiracy and force someone in Lordaeron's command structure to take the warning seriously.
constraints: Limited Third Absolute — Jaina's perception only
Do not identify the recurring trader clue beyond repetition and suspicion
Arthas's dismissal must read as visible-war priority and hierarchy, not simple personal cruelty

povKnowsAtOpen: The network is organized, monitored, and disciplined
Barov infrastructure appears in the route fragments
The party's chosen road intersects the military zone near Strahnbrad before reaching Andorhal
Her sensitivity has put them ahead of what most of Lordaeron understands, but they are still chasing a deployment already in motion rather than preventing it
The old seller from Millhaven is still sitting in memory as an unresolved grain-side oddity
She can see this was built on purpose and tied to necromancy, but she still cannot explain exactly how sickness becomes undeath
povDoesNotKnowAtOpen: Whether the Barovs are fully complicit
What Andorhal looks like from inside the contamination radius
mustNotBeImpliedYet: No certainty that the Barovs themselves designed the plague
No suggestion that Arthas dismisses Jaina out of personal cruelty alone; his visible-war priority matters
castKnowsLeavingChapter: Arthas heard the warning and still chose the visible war first
The repeated farewell has sharpened the old seller file for Sylvanas without resolving it
The party reaches a still-working Andorhal at the heart of the plague roads

pov.currentStanceAtOpen: If Jaina can lay the case out cleanly enough, a prince standing on his own soil will have to act.
pov.surfaceObjective: Make Arthas understand the plague before Andorhal locks itself beyond saving.
pov.pressureSource: The warning is arriving inside rank, pride, local denial, and a prince who wants a cleaner answer than the road has given them.
pov.misbeliefManifestation: Jaina keeps thinking clarity should win on its own, even when the room keeps showing her that people are choosing hierarchy over evidence.
pov.chapterTest: Arthas hears the case and still treats it like something he can out-command or out-delay.
pov.forcedChoice: Keep pushing the clean explanation she wants to believe should work, or accept Sylvanas's harder read of the room and start planning past official refusal.
pov.endStateShift: Jaina leaves not just furious but corrected. Being right did nothing by itself.
pov.carryForwardResidue: Chapter 7 opens with Jaina quicker to believe the city, not the argument, will tell the truth first.

throughPov.visibleFunction: The woman who reads people, rank, and refusal faster than Jaina does.
throughPov.povMisreadAtOpen: Jaina no longer underrates Sylvanas's mind, but she still half hopes clear evidence can outrun Sylvanas's cynicism.
throughPov.correctionEarnedHere: Sylvanas is not being cynical. She is reading command blindness in real time, and she sees the refusal before Jaina stops arguing with it.
throughPov.interactionRule: Jaina can be openly sharp with Sylvanas now, but the sharpness should sound like friction between peers, not condescension.

team.label: partnership
team.operatingModeAtOpen: Working partners under strain, still leaning on proof more than politics.
team.operationalChangeThisChapter: Their contempt finally points outward at the same problem. By the end, they are acting against refusal, not arguing about whether refusal exists.
team.namingAttitudeRule: Jaina's respect is real enough now that disagreement should not flatten back into soldier-versus-mage condescension.
team.doNotOverstateRule: They are not easy with each other. They are simply more aligned than the people with titles around them.

entries.rule: Not with the party; she remains on the northbound warning line after escorting the survivor girl out.

castAndHandoffRules.chapterHandoffTarget: Chapter 7 opens at Andorhal with the same four riders carrying Arthas's dismissal, the repeated farewell clue, and the road that brought them there.

factsLockedForChapter7: Arthas has heard the warning and not acted on it in the needed way
The repeated farewell is now a remembered pattern for Sylvanas
The party reaches Andorhal via the road chosen in Chapter 5
The city still looks alive and busy even though the plague roads all run through it
factsNotYetEarned: Any claim that human authorities are now fully useless everywhere rather than specifically failing here

scenes.title: Conspiracy Crystallizes
scenes.sceneFunction: Make the plague read as deliberate war, not just a string of bad villages.

enrichment.detail: The plague mechanism's grain layer is only the outer shell; repeated ingestion conditions the body and explains why Jaina can now see elegant, deliberate design without yet solving the final undeath lock. That lets her reaction here read as professional recognition plus horror, not sudden omniscience.

beats.goal: Reconcile the evidence into one stable explanation before they hit the city
beats.conflict: Jaina has to admit the ugly elegance of it: alchemy, necromancy, road traffic, and ordinary bread all folded together

outcome.summary: The field model coheres, but the clarity makes the enemy feel more capable, not less.

beats.valueShift: chosen road -> the pattern finally stands plain
newOnPageInformation: The pattern is too clean for accident or lone-wizard sabotage
The living body is part of the design, not just the thing being poisoned
A trained magical mind sits somewhere behind the work
stillUnknownAfterBeat: How many layers of organization sit between village watchers and command
sensoryAnchors: south-feeder road beginning to thicken with troop churn before Strahnbrad
beats.transitionIntent: analysis-pressure: once the model coheres, Jaina has to sit with what it says about the mind behind it
beats.verifierNotes: Build from the already-established grain-road theorem; do not present ingestion or regional scale as a fresh discovery.
beats.verifierModification: Added early war-road pressure to the opening sensory line so chapter 5's route-choice payoff stays active before the Strahnbrad scene lands.

beats.dilemma: Jaina can either deny the brilliance in the work or admit that the work is monstrously well-made
beats.decision: Admit the quality privately and keep moving
beats.valueShift: the pattern finally stands plain -> the enemy is brilliant, and that is worse
newOnPageInformation: Jaina recognizes methodology she would admire in any non-genocidal context
The work is pressing against banned Kirin Tor questions about what a body can be taught to keep through death
Her revulsion is sharpened by professional recognition
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether this mirror points to someone she already knows
beats.transitionIntent: world-collision: the road then delivers them into Lordaeron's visible war, which competes with the invisible one
beats.verifierNotes: Jaina's professional recognition is an escalation of her existing necromantic read, not sudden omniscience.

scenes.title: Strahnbrad — The Prince
scenes.sceneFunction: Take a true warning to a prince who has no room for it.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The plague picture is now clear to the protagonists
Lordaeron command has not yet absorbed it
scenes.arcPressure: Jaina thinks clarity should win. Strahnbrad forces her to watch rank, urgency, and old personal history beat evidence in real time.

enrichment.detail: Strahnbrad sits in the northeastern Hillsbrad foothills near Alterac, and Warcraft III frames it as the site of Blackrock-orc raids, captive villagers, and civilian defense pressure. Arthas therefore arrives as the golden prince already inside a mud-and-banners war frame, primed to prioritize the threat he can see and swing at.
enrichment.source: Warcraft Wiki: Strahnbrad; Warcraft Wiki: The Defense of Strahnbrad (WC3 Human); stories/the-plague-road/characters/arthas.md

beats.goal: Reach a Lordaeron authority figure who can act on the warning
beats.conflict: Strahnbrad is fully committed to the Blackrock-orc emergency, which is immediate, visible, and politically legible in ways the plague is not

outcome.summary: They find Arthas exactly where the chosen road said they would, but he is already embedded in another crisis frame.

beats.valueShift: the enemy is brilliant, and that is worse -> the warning has to compete with visible war
newOnPageInformation: The Chapter 5 route choice paid off geographically
Arthas is here because Lordaeron is spending force on the threat it can already see
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether personal history will help or hinder Jaina's warning
beats.transitionIntent: audience-pull: Jaina gets her chance to speak, but not on terms she controls
beats.verifierNotes: Strahnbrad is new on-page, but the collision with Arthas's theater is a payoff from the chapter 5 route choice, not a random detour.

beats.goal: Make Arthas act on the grain warning
beats.conflict: Arthas is courteous, focused, and looking at the wrong fire: he is handling the war in front of him and hears the plague as a secondary problem

outcome.summary: Jaina delivers correct intelligence, and Arthas still dismisses it in practice.

beats.valueShift: the warning has to compete with visible war -> being right changes nothing
newOnPageInformation: Arthas hears the warning and chooses the visible threat first
The personal history sharpens the wound but is not the sole reason for dismissal
Jaina can feel Sylvanas reading Arthas's priorities and blind spots faster than the briefing itself is landing
stillUnknownAfterBeat: How much damage this delay will cause downstream
beats.transitionIntent: emotional-fallout: once Arthas leaves, Jaina has to absorb both the personal wound and the operational failure
beats.verifierNotes: Arthas is first on-page here. Keep the old wound as new context inside Jaina, and keep the refusal institutional rather than petty.

beats.dilemma: Read Arthas's dismissal as private failure, public failure, or both
beats.decision: Treat it as both and keep moving
beats.valueShift: being right changes nothing -> contempt sharpened into clarity
newOnPageInformation: Jaina understands that being right about Arthas years ago and being right about the plague now produce the same useless result when the listener will not change
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether any human authority will act before disaster goes public
beats.transitionIntent: alignment-pull: Sylvanas now answers the refusal as a commander problem, not a romantic one
beats.verifierNotes: This is the first explicit tie between Arthas and Jaina's older wound; do not frame it as recap the reader already has.

beats.valueShift: contempt sharpened into clarity -> shared outward contempt
newOnPageInformation: Sylvanas instantly reads the exchange without needing the whole history
Jaina and Sylvanas can now align against the same blind spot in another person
Jaina chooses to treat Sylvanas's faster read as tactical correction and turn the quartet toward proof instead of sanction
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Andorhal will give them proof strong enough to force action later
sensoryAnchors: Arthas gone but still shaping the air after him
Sylvanas's short diagnosis cutting cleaner than comfort would
horses turning back toward the road under Jaina's decision to keep moving
beats.transitionIntent: self-directed-pursuit: Jaina accepts the refusal as final and turns the quartet back onto the road, making Andorhal the next proof target
beats.dilemma: Keep trying to win sanction from Arthas, or accept the refusal and make Andorhal the proof target that has to speak for itself.
beats.decision: Accept the refusal, let Sylvanas's faster read stand, and turn the quartet back toward Andorhal without waiting for permission.
beats.verifierNotes: Keep the alignment hard and tactical. Jaina is taking a strategic correction, not reaching for comfort.
beats.verifierModification: Retyped this beat from a scene/disaster into a decision sequel so Jaina owns the pivot after Arthas's refusal instead of being passively corrected.

scenes.title: The Approach to Andorhal
scenes.sceneFunction: Carry the Arthas failure forward, reintroduce the old seller pattern as a repeat clue, and put the city on the horizon.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Arthas will not solve this for them

enrichment.detail: Andorhal is Lordaeron's breadbasket and main agricultural distribution point in the Eastweald, with active granaries, wagon traffic, and outwardly normal commerce. The right first look is therefore a working grain city whose surface competence makes the hidden contamination feel worse.

beats.goal: Gather final directional intelligence on the city approach
beats.conflict: Another helpful grain-side local gives them exactly the information they need and parts with the same odd farewell

outcome.summary: The direction is useful, and the repeated phrase now snags harder on Sylvanas's memory without becoming actionable proof.

beats.valueShift: shared outward contempt -> filed suspicion with pattern
newOnPageInformation: The farewell is now a repetition, not a one-off oddity
Sylvanas has a real memory hook, even if not an answer
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Why the same phrase is recurring
beats.transitionIntent: physical-pressure: once the exchange ends, the road itself starts announcing the city's proximity
beats.verifierNotes: Sharpen the recurring breadcrumb into a pattern, but do not imply the identity is solved or that the repeated speaker is proven to be the same man.

beats.dilemma: Push through the pain of the corruption field now that the source is almost visible, or stop and recover
beats.valueShift: filed suspicion with pattern -> the city is close enough to hurt
newOnPageInformation: Jaina's body is now reacting to proximity, not just reading it
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Andorhal is already too late to save
beats.transitionIntent: arrival-hook: the chapter closes only once the city itself is in sight
beats.verifierNotes: Jaina's bodily backlash is established; intensify it rather than treating magical pain as a brand-new symptom.

beats.goal: Reach visual confirmation of the tightest active grain hub they have narrowed down so far
beats.conflict: The city is still busy and working, which makes the contamination feel embedded at distribution scale rather than isolated to ruined villages.

outcome.summary: They have reached the tightest active knot in the network so far, and it is still operating as though nothing is wrong.

beats.valueShift: the city is close enough to hurt -> the network's busiest visible hub is alive and unaware of its own danger
newOnPageInformation: Andorhal is not a ruin; it is a working grain city carrying the plague in plain sight.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Who inside the city is running the thing
Whether Andorhal is the ultimate origin or only the largest visible knot they can currently reach
beats.transitionIntent: chapter-handoff: Chapter 7 opens inside the Andorhal investigation with official refusal still hot and the city's surface normalcy now on-page
beats.verifierNotes: Andorhal is already an established target. The new beat is the first direct sight of a functioning grain city, not a fresh place-name reveal.
beats.verifierModification: Reduced Andorhal from a claimed source city to the tightest active hub yet, preserving chapter 5's rule that it is the next knot in the network, not final proof of ultimate origin.

normalizations: Expanded markdown beat labels into explicit beatType and scenePhase fields for all 9 beats.
Preserved the chapter-close handoff as structured locks for Chapter 7 instead of flattening it into summary text.
Trimmed the Scene 3 trader cast label to a reader-facing role so the recurring clue stays suggestive rather than explicitly identified.
warnings: Beats 1-5 darken in the same general direction before beat 6 converts rejection into alignment, so the chapter's front half runs on deliberate downward pressure.
Scene outcomes skew narrow: 4 yes-but and 2 no-and, with no clean yes. The fit is acceptable for a refusal chapter, but there is little uncomplicated relief.

chapterBridge: Direct carry from chapter 5's feeder-road commitment: the same four riders spend the opening miles on the chosen south road, carrying the Barov clue, the unresolved grain-seller breadcrumb, and the expectation that Strahnbrad's war traffic is now part of the route rather than a detour.

craftResearch.findings: Anchor clue synthesis to action or decision, filter revelations through POV, and let every answer widen the threat instead of closing it.
craftResearch.source: Web search synthesis of Quill & Steel, Writers Digest, and Jane Friedman craft guidance

craftResearch.findings: Make the refusal fair by giving the authority a legible competing priority; let the scene pivot the protagonist into a new course instead of ending in static frustration.
craftResearch.source: Web search synthesis of StudioBinder, Helping Writers Become Authors, Jerry Jenkins, and Jane Friedman

craftResearch.findings: Introduce the city through surface order plus subtle dissonance, and let sensory detail register danger before the chapter explains it.
craftResearch.source: Web search synthesis of scene-tension and pacing craft articles

pacing.notes: The chapter rises from analytical unease to the Arthas refusal spike, breathes in beat 6, then lands on the Andorhal hook. The front half stays deliberately downward in mood, but the sequel beats keep it from flattening.

structure.notes: The mid-chapter turn lands at Arthas's refusal. Beat 6 was retyped into a decision sequel so Jaina, not the room, owns the pivot from seeking sanction to seeking proof. Beat 9 was cut back from source certainty to the largest visible knot yet, keeping chapter 7 room to discover more.

character.notes: Jaina stays in absolute POV and retains agency in every sequence. Sylvanas functions as the faster room-reader without stealing the chapter turn, and the partnership shift stays tactical rather than soft.

transitions.notes: All 9 beat-level transitionIntent chains were verified. No transition gaps required new intents, but beat 6's handoff was strengthened so the chapter exits refusal and re-enters pursuit under Jaina's decision. Chapter bridge now carries chapter 5's route payoff directly into the opening road pressure.

continuity.notes: 2 beats are new and 7 are evolutions. Fixed 1 continuity overclaim by reframing Andorhal as the tightest active hub rather than proven source, and corrected 3 anti-reintroduction risks around the grain theorem, the recurring farewell, and Andorhal's prior establishment.

modifications.description: Added early war-road pressure to beat 1 so chapter 5's south-feeder-road payoff stays active before Strahnbrad proper appears.
Retyped beat 6 from a scene/disaster into a decision sequel so Jaina chooses the pivot from failed sanction to Andorhal-as-proof.
Reduced beat 9 from source certainty to the tightest active hub yet, preserving prior memory that Andorhal was the next knot in the network, not final proof of origin.
Annotated all 9 beats with continuityStatus, added memoryRef on every evolution beat, built requiredMemory, and attached continuity-only verifier notes where anti-reintroduction risk was highest.
